channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Wrexham says one of its players hospitalized after road incident on way home from match
item.description: WREXHAM, Wales (AP) — Wrexham midfielder Elliot Lee was taken to the hospital after being involved in what the Welsh club described as a “road traffic incident” on the way home from a match, the team said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former Lia Thomas teammate calls out Democrats still fighting for trans athletes in women's sports
item.description: Former UPenn swimmer Grace Estabrook tells Fox News Digital that elected officials still fighting to protect trans inclusion in women's sports is "depressing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bellingham strikes late for Real Madrid in 3-2 win at Man City in Champions League 1st leg playoff
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Real Madrid’s Galacticos just don’t know when they are beaten. Manchester City just keep on making the same mistakes. Jude Bellingham’s stoppage-time goal sealed a stunning comeback for Madrid and a 3-2 lead in its Champions League playoff against City.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Eagles' roster overhaul between two Super Bowl titles is an NFL rarity
item.description: Seven years after Doug Pederson and Nick Foles delivered Philadelphia its first Super Bowl title, Nick Sirianni and Jalen Hurts did it again with a 40-22 win over Kansas City in Super Bowl 59.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dubow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UConn's Liam McNeeley draws inspiration for 38-point performance from Creighton fans heckling Hurley
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — UConn freshman star Liam McNeeley hadn't played in front of a crowd as hostile as the one the Huskies faced in Omaha on Tuesday night. He found the environment inspiring.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: De Beque's Burke Largent scores 1,000th point as Dragon fans go crazy at Caprock
item.description: As Largent eclipsed the 1K mark on a tough bucket over three defenders, the scores of royal blue in the Caprock stands went absolutely berserk.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Hall of Famer Steve Young weighs in on Aaron Rodgers' NFL future as possible divorce with Jets looms
item.description: Aaron Rodgers' future with the New York Jets appears to be up in the air, as recent reports stated the franchise is ready to going in a different direction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chicago State begins search for head football coach
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The South Side's Chicago State University launched a search to find their first head football coach Tuesday. “We are excited to reach this milestone in our football planning.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Eagles' DeVonta Smith reaches rare milestones in Super Bowl LIX victory
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles wide receiver DeVonta Smith became the fifth player in NFL history to win the Heisman, a national tile, and the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yankees' Boone says he hopes his team would handle title `with a little more class' than Dodgers
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Three months after the World Series, Yankees manager Aaron Boone remained unhappy with some of the Los Angeles Dodgers' remarks after they beat a sloppy New York team in five games for the title.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NASCAR drivers divided on new rule that could guarantee Helio Castroneves a spot in the Daytona 500
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — A controversial new NASCAR rule designed for “world-class drivers" could give Helio Castroneves an automatic berth into the Daytona 500, and, should the four-time Indianapolis 500 winner use the provisional, it would make for the largest field in a decade.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Matthew Stafford, Giants rumors swirl after team’s latest coaching move
item.description: The New York Giants hired Matthew Stafford's brother-in-law as assistant quarterbacks coach as trade rumors swirl around the team acquiring the veteran QB.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clayton Kershaw to return to Los Angeles Dodgers: reports
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers legend Clayton Kershaw is returning to the defending World Series champions for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Cowboys star CeeDee Lamb sounds off amid teammate Micah Parsons trade rumors
item.description: The Dallas Cowboys 2024 was injury-riddled and disappointing, but star pass rusher Micah Parsons delivered another double-digit sack season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NBA All-Star Game salutes Bay Area music scene with great lineup of talent
item.description: E-40, En Vogue, Saweetie among Bay Area stars set to appear at NBA All-Star Game festivities at Golden State Warriors' Chase Center in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Jim Harrington, The Mercury News

item.title: Guy Gaudreau joins US team for practice at the 4 Nations Face-Off
item.description: MONTREAL (AP) — Guy Gaudreau spoke softly, cracked a joke about how bad a hockey player he was and nodded his head with appreciation. On the lapel of his jacket was a pin honoring the memory of his sons, John and Matt.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Champions League global rights deal set to be struck by U.S. agency Relevent with UEFA, clubs
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — UEFA and top European soccer clubs are set to drop the Swiss agency that has sold Champions League rights since 1992 and replace it with the U.S. firm of Miami Dolphins owner Stephen Ross.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lakers blow past Jazz in Doncic debut, 132-113
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz were no match for the Lakers in the much-anticipated debut of Luka Doncic. Doncic had 14 points, five rebounds and four assists in his Lakers debut as Los Angeles beat the Utah Jazz 132-113 on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Super Bowl returning to the Bay Area for 2026
item.description: (KRON) -- The Super Bowl is coming back to the Bay Area next year. A decade ago, the region hosted Super Bowl 50. Next year, it will play host to Super Bowl LX. On Monday morning, San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan was in New Orleans for the official Super Bowl handoff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Rudd, Edwards named newest NASCAR Hall of Fame inductees
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — One of the toughest drivers in NASCAR history joined one the most enigmatic drivers of the modern era as the newest inductees into the Hall of Fame in a star-studded Friday night ceremony.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ex-MLB player Jacque Jones has high school jersey retired
item.description: Jacque Jones, a former Major League Baseball outfielder and San Diego native, had his legacy cemented in his alma mater's history on Saturday with the official retirement of his jersey number.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Anthony Davis is out with groin strain for second game with Dallas Mavericks amid reports of longer absence
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis has been ruled out of what would have been his second game with the Mavericks amid reports that a groin injury sustained in his Dallas debut could sideline him for multiple weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Super Bowl LIX highlights: Memorable plays, moving tributes and a historic presidential visit
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in the first Super Bowl to be held in New Orleans in a dozen years. It was played amid heavy security — and with President Donald Trump in attendance — just 41 days after a man plowed a truck into New Year's revelers on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the top 5 commercials from the Super Bowl
item.description: some people may not have been watching this year’s Super Bowl just for the football or rap superstar Kendrick Lamar’s mid-game performance.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Eagles running back Saquon Barkley sets the NFL's season rushing record, including the playoffs
item.description: Saquon Barkley set an NFL single-season rushing record in the Super Bowl, held his young daughter as a torrent of confetti fluttered around them and flashed a smile as he held a Philadelphia newspaper with “CHAMPS!” stamped above his photo.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Did you know? The flag presented during the national anthem at Super Bowl LIX came from Utah
item.description: The flag presented during the performance of the national anthem at Super Bowl LIX was handmade by a Utah-based flag company.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: Celebrities spotted at the Super Bowl
item.description: The players on the field Sunday at Super Bowl LIX are celebrities in their own right, but they're far from the only big names gathered in New Orleans for the Chiefs-Eagles matchup.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Secret Service unveils Super Bowl ad
item.description: “America was founded on an idea of freedom. America's always stepped forward in time of need throughout our short but powerful history,” says a voiceover in the video.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Blackhawks take losing streak into matchup with the Predators
item.description: Nashville Predators (18-27-7, in the Central Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (16-31-6, in the Central Division) Chicago; Friday, 8:30 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks play the Nashville Predators after losing three straight games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: SDCC coach battles cancer while coaching
item.description: Jose Martinez's whole life is basketball, from playing on the Puerto Rican national team at 15 years old, to becoming an assistant coach at San Diego City College.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Daytona boss inspires students with his NASCAR journey
item.description: The "Super Bowl" of racing, the Daytona 500, will be the next biggest event in the world of sports, taking place February 16, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Davis Suppes, NewsNation

item.title: Madsen brothers lead Utes past Colorado, 72-59
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Gabe Madsen scored 17 points, Lawson Lovering scored 12 points and Utah kept Colorado winless in the Big 12 beating the Buffaloes 72-59 on Wednesday night. Ezra Ausar and reserve Mason Madsen each scored 10 points for the Utes (13-9, 5-6 Big 12).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Report: Bulls, Ball agree to contract extension
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bulls and point guard Lonzo Ball have agreed to a two-year contract extension, according to ESPN's Shams Charania. Charania reports the deal is worth $20 million over two years.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Bucs hire former USF head coach Charlie Strong as defensive line coach
item.description: According to NFL Network's Ian Rapoport, the Bucs have hired former USF head coach Charlie Strong to be their new defensive line coach.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, WFLA, NBC
